topic_title: Reading from a file — open, read lines, close
verdict: PASS — all checks clean. TD flowchart with 6 nodes correctly maps the file-reading lifecycle. Open → Read → branch (close how?) → Manual (.close()) / Preferred (with block) → file closed. Labels are plain-language, no unexplained acronyms, all concepts within topic scope. Preferred path visually distinguished (green, ★ edge label). PNG renders cleanly at 30,456 bytes with no overlap or truncation.

label_clarity.notes: All node labels are plain-language and self-explanatory. 'open(file.txt, r) returns a file object', 'read the content .read() • .readlines() • for line in f:', 'close how?', 'f.close() manual — easy to forget', 'with block closed automatically at end', 'file closed ✓ resources freed'. No bare acronyms. Edge labels 'manual pattern' and 'preferred ★' are unambiguous.

scope_discipline.notes: All 6 nodes represent 12.6 concepts exclusively: open(), three read methods (.read/.readlines/for-line), the close decision, manual .close(), with block, and terminal state. No advanced/adjacent material (write mode, try/except, CSV, binary files) appears.

intent_coverage.intent: Flowchart of the file-reading lifecycle: open() → read content → close, with the with block shown as the auto-close alternative path vs manual .close()
intent_coverage.notes: Lifecycle shown in order. Branch at 'close how?' cleanly separates the two paths. with block labeled as 'preferred ★', manual path labeled 'easy to forget' — reinforces the best-practice message without prose.

visual_render.notes: PNG renders cleanly. No text truncation, no node overlap, no arrow crossings. Color coding legible: blue = process steps, yellow = decision diamond, green = preferred path, purple-blue stadium = terminal.

beginner_clarity.notes: Preferred path (with block) visually distinguished via color (green) and edge label (★). Manual path annotated with 'easy to forget' — directly addresses the common beginner mistake. Terminal node 'file closed ✓ resources freed' explains why closing matters.
